objective: Demonstrate deterministic changed-policy replay over recorded mediated events and a separately labeled fresh Firecracker/Codex scenario rerun, with explicit comparability, permitted divergence, repeated freshness, and teardown proof.
assumptions: Policy replay evaluates only the fixed projected fields already present in verified host-mediated events and never reopens quarantine.
Fresh scenario rerun equality is required only for scenario/tool identity; execution identity, timing, artifacts, model output/actions, and bounded verdict are explicitly permitted to differ.
The current provider is scripted and deterministic, but the scenario operation remains labeled fresh and nondeterministic to preserve correct live-model semantics.
A changed policy does not retroactively establish how a model would have reasoned and replay makes no such counterfactual claim.
Two fresh Phase 5 chamber executions plus prior repeated/timeout tests provide the bounded stale-state and orphan-cleanup sample for this target.

commands_executed.concise_output: Two Phase 5 tests passed in 14.101 seconds, including two fresh real microVM runs.

commands_executed.command: retained observe source run, deterministic replay to capability-bound, and fresh capability-bound scenario rerun
commands_executed.concise_output: Source run_ffa01a581c630f87 and rerun run_02ed6d18327a66d1 had distinct canary digests; scenario identity equal; policy changed; both teardown checks true.
29 tests passed in 41.114 seconds; static and negative checks passed; both images clean; mutable run root empty.

tests_and_falsifiers.test: Identical recorded events and target policy yield identical replay record and hash
Recorded observe-mode diagnostic action deterministically re-evaluates to BLOCK
Replay-record mutation fails replay hash verification
Policy replay executes no Firecracker or model process
Replay artifact can be hash-bound into a derived envelope with previous-envelope link
Fresh rerun receives distinct run ID and canary digest
Fresh rerun preserves scenario and tool-surface hashes and declares policy change
Current live-model divergence is explicitly NOT_CHECKED_LIVE_MODEL
Mutated scenario identity is rejected as undeclared rerun drift
Both Firecracker PIDs are gone and shared mutable run directory is empty

limitations_and_checks_not_run: Policy replay does not re-execute a model or effect and makes no counterfactual model-behavior claim.
The scenario rerun uses SCRIPTED_MOCK_PROVIDER; live-model nondeterminism and divergence remain NOT_CHECKED_LIVE_MODEL.
The retained replay source contained one package_lookup action; the diagnostic OBSERVE-to-BLOCK change is proven by the separately labeled deterministic fixture test.
A two-run Phase 5 sample plus prior phase runs is a bounded cleanup test, not proof against every stale-state or host-kernel failure mode.
No global safety or causal claim is made.
